Employees.question: Get employee first and last names
Get employees ordered by last name
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees ORDER BY last_name ASC;

Employees.question: Get top 5 highest paid employees
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5;

Employees.question: Get employees with salary greater than 50000
Get employees whose first name starts with A
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE first_name LIKE 'A%';

Employees.question: Get the highest salary in the company
Get the lowest salary in the company
Get number of employees per department
Employees.answer: SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Employees GROUP BY department_id;

Departments.question: Get departments ordered by name
Departments.answer: SELECT * FROM Departments ORDER BY name ASC;

Departments.question: Get employees with their department name
Departments.answer: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.name AS department FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id;

Departments.question: Get average salary per department
Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.name;

Departments.question: Get departments with more than 5 employees
Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(e.id) > 5;

Departments.question: Get departments with no employees
Departments.answer: SELECT * FROM Departments WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT department_id FROM Employees);

Departments.question: Get the department with the highest total salary
Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, SUM(e.salary) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1;

Employees_Departments.question: Get each employee with their department name
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.name AS department FROM Employees e INNER JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id;

Employees_Departments.question: Get all departments and their employees (including departments with no employees)
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.name AS department, e.first_name, e.last_name FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Employees e ON d.id = e.department_id;

Employees_Departments.question: Get all employees including those with no department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.name AS department FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id;

Employees_Departments.question: Get average salary per department name
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.name;

Employees_Departments.question: Get departments with more than 3 employees
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(e.id) > 3;

Employees_Departments.question: Get the highest paid employee in each department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE e.salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM Employees WHERE department_id = e.department_id);

Employees_Departments.question: Get total salary cost per department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.name, SUM(e.salary) AS total_salary FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY total_salary DESC;

Employees_Departments.question: Get employees who earn more than their department average
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary FROM Employees e WHERE e.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM Employees WHERE department_id = e.department_id);

Customers.question: Get customers ordered by name
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers ORDER BY name ASC;

Customers.question: Get customers from a specific city
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE city = 'New York';

Customers.question: Get distinct cities customers are from
Get customers whose name contains 'son'
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE name LIKE '%son%';

Customers.question: Get customers who signed up after 2022
Count customers per city
Customers.answer: SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Customers GROUP BY city;

Customers.question: Get the most recently signed up customer
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers ORDER BY signup_date DESC LIMIT 1;

Orders.question: Get orders ordered by date descending
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders ORDER BY order_date DESC;

Orders.question: Get total revenue from all orders
Get orders with amount greater than 100
Get orders placed in 2024
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-12-31';

Orders.question: Get number of orders per customer
Orders.answer: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total_orders FROM Orders GROUP BY customer_id;

Orders.question: Get customers with their orders
Orders.answer: SELECT c.name, o.id AS order_id, o.total_amount FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;

Orders.question: Get customers who have more than 3 orders
Orders.answer: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;

Orders.question: Get the customer who spent the most
Orders.answer: SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) AS total_spent FROM Orders GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;

Orders.question: Get orders with no matching customer
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM Customers);

Orders.question: Get total revenue per customer
Orders.answer: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name;

Orders.question: Get orders sorted by amount descending
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders ORDER BY total_amount DESC;
